title: Cloudflare Free Upload Limit
fact: Cloudflare's Free plan has a hard upload limit of 100MB per request. This is a common cause for '413 Request Entity Too Large' errors when importing large backups.
solution: Temporarily 'Pause Cloudflare' or switch the DNS record to 'DNS Only' (Grey Cloud) during the upload process, then re-enable the proxy after completion.

title: WP-Cron Performance Hit
fact: WordPress's default 'WP-Cron' fires on every page load. On high-traffic sites, this can significantly slow down response times and cause database locks.
solution: Disable WP-Cron in wp-config.php and set up a real system Cron Job (via cPanel or SSH) to hit wp-cron.php every 5-15 minutes instead.

title: Admin Ajax & Heartbeat
fact: The WordPress Heartbeat API sends AJAX calls every 15-60 seconds in the dashboard. This can consume 100% of CPU on shared hosting if multiple windows are left open.
solution: Use the 'Heartbeat Control' plugin or a custom snippet to limit frequency or disable it entirely where it's not needed.
